provenance.source_corpus: Twenty years of ecosystem response after clearcutting and slashburning in conifer forests of central British Columbia, Canada

source_spans.text: Twenty years of ecosystem response after clearcutting and slashburning in conifer forests of central British Columbia, Canada
Twenty years post-treatment, species diversity increased in all zones
plant abundance was greatest in the Interior Cedar-Hemlock

nodes.name: cover of understory plant functional types associated with mature conifer forests

source_spans.text: Cover of understory plant functional types associated with mature conifer forests increased in all zones

nodes.name: understory species diagnostic of mature forests

source_spans.text: Understory species diagnostic of mature forests were present in all zones by year 20

nodes.name: loss of compositional or functional diversity

source_spans.text: Overall, loss of compositional or functional diversity following harvest and site remediation was not detected
suggesting that montane and subalpine forests in British Columbia are resilient to this treatment

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:5057a7a686c6
edges.original_sentence: Cover of understory plant functional types associated with mature conifer forests increased in all zones, constituting a significant proportion (> 40%) of the vegetation community by year 20.

source_spans.text: Cover of understory plant functional types associated with mature conifer forests increased in all zones, constituting a significant proportion (> 40%) of the vegetation community by year 20.

source_document.title: Twenty years of ecosystem response after clearcutting and slashburning in conifer forests of central British Columbia, Canada

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:5057a7a686c6
edges.original_sentence: Understory species diagnostic of mature forests were present in all zones by year 20, but we identified indicator species sensitive to slashburning or requiring more time for recovery, including white-flowered rhododendron (Rhododendron albiflorum) and devil’s club (Oplopanax horridus).

source_spans.text: Understory species diagnostic of mature forests were present in all zones by year 20, but we identified indicator species sensitive to slashburning or requiring more time for recovery, including white-flowered rhododendron (Rhododendron albiflorum) and devil’s club (Oplopanax horridus).

source_document.title: Twenty years of ecosystem response after clearcutting and slashburning in conifer forests of central British Columbia, Canada

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:5057a7a686c6
edges.original_sentence: Overall, loss of compositional or functional diversity following harvest and site remediation was not detected, suggesting that montane and subalpine forests in British Columbia are resilient to this treatment.
